questions.question: If the polynomial $p(x) = x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10$ is divided by another polynomial $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$, the remainder comes out to be $x + a$. Find the values of $k$ and $a$, and identify the quotient of the division.

steps: Perform long division of $p(x)$ by $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$.
The first term of quotient is $x^2$. Multiplying $g(x)$ by $x^2$ gives $x^4 - 2x^3 + kx^2$. Subtracting from $p(x)$ gives $-4x^3 + (16-k)x^2 - 25x$.
The second term of quotient is $-4x$. Multiplying $g(x)$ by $-4x$ gives $-4x^3 + 8x^2 - 4kx$. Subtracting this gives $((16-k)-8)x^2 + (-25+4k)x + 10 = (8-k)x^2 + (4k-25)x + 10$.
The third term of quotient is $(8-k)$. Multiplying $g(x)$ by $(8-k)$ gives $(8-k)x^2 - 2(8-k)x + k(8-k)$.
Subtracting this from the current remainder results in the final remainder: $[(4k-25) - (-16+2k)]x + [10 - (8k-k^2)] = (2k-9)x + (k^2-8k+10)$.
Equate the coefficients of the remainder $(2k-9)x + (k^2-8k+10)$ to the given remainder $x + a$.
Solve $2k-9 = 1$ to find $k=5$. Then substitute $k=5$ into $a = k^2-8k+10$ to find $a = 25 - 40 + 10 = -5$.
solution.derivation: $x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10 = (x^2 - 2x + k)(x^2 - 4x + (8-k)) + (2k-9)x + (k^2-8k+10) \ \text{Given remainder is } x + a \ \Rightarrow 2k-9 = 1 \Rightarrow 2k = 10 \Rightarrow k = 5 \ \Rightarrow a = k^2 - 8k + 10 = (5)^2 - 8(5) + 10 = 25 - 40 + 10 = -5 \ \text{Quotient} = x^2 - 4x + (8-5) = x^2 - 4x + 3$
